NADZ Autonomic Control™ combines NESA® XSIGNAL® microcurrent neuromodulation with our in-home clinical expertise to help rebalance the autonomic nervous system (ANS), supporting recovery, performance and long-term wellness. Non-invasive. Sub-sensory. No drugs. No needles. No downtime. Just gentle, science-led autonomic support — delivered at home.

bullets: Male sexual function (erection & ejaculation performance — functional component)

journey.title: What to Expect During Your Home Session

steps.title: SCREENING & HISTORY (5–10 MIN)
steps.description: Your NADZ physiotherapist or doctor evaluates suitability and goals.

steps.title: ELECTRODE PLACEMENT (3–5 MIN)
steps.description: Non-invasive electrodes are placed on wrists and ankles.

steps.title: SUB-SENSORY MICROCURRENT SESSION (40–60 MIN)
steps.description: You remain fully clothed, seated or resting. Many clients choose to combine it with physiotherapy post-session.

steps.title: REVIEW & PLAN (5–10 MIN)
steps.description: Your doctor or physiotherapist provides guidance based on your goals and response.

safety.title: Safety & Contraindications
safety.description: NADZ Autonomic Control™ is: ✔ Non-invasive ✔ Not felt by most individuals ✔ Conducted by trained clinicians. Contraindications include: Pregnancy (particularly the first trimester), Incompatible implanted cardiac devices, Active open wounds on electrode sites. Your doctor or physiotherapist will perform a complete screening prior to therapy.

faqs.q: Is the therapy painful?
faqs.a: No. The microcurrents are below the sensory threshold — most feel no sensation at all.

faqs.q: Can this replace physiotherapy or medical treatment?
faqs.a: No. It is a complementary therapy intended to support autonomic balance, making other treatments more effective.

faqs.q: How many sessions do I need?
faqs.a: This depends on your goals. Many start with a structured weekly plan and adjust based on progress.

faqs.q: Are there side effects?
faqs.a: Adverse effects are uncommon. Your doctor will perform full screening.

faqs.q: Can it help with erectile dysfunction or premature ejaculation?
faqs.a: NADZ Autonomic Control™ supports the autonomic (functional) component of sexual performance.

faqs.q: Do I need HRV testing?
faqs.a: No. HRV is optional and used for educational insight, not diagnosis.

faqs.q: Can I combine this with physiotherapy or IV drips?
faqs.a: Yes. Physiotherapy immediately after a session is often beneficial.

faqs.q: Is it safe for elderly or neurological patients?
faqs.a: Yes, It is suitable after screening with our physiotherapists under our doctor's supervision.

cta.tagline: Your journey to autonomic balance starts here.

Each peptide is carefully selected based on your biology, your goals, and your diagnostic markers — ensuring maximum efficacy, safety and personalization.

whatIs.title: What Is Peptide Therapy?
whatIs.paragraph: Peptides are short chains of amino acids that act as messengers in the body — communicating with cells, activating repair mechanisms, regulating hormones, modulating inflammation, and supporting tissue regeneration.
whatIs.footer: Because they are smaller than full proteins, peptides are highly bioavailable, fast-acting, and extremely targeted, making them ideal for functional, regenerative, and longevity medicine.

items.description: Supports musculoskeletal recovery, post-injury healing, joint repair, and soft tissue regeneration.

faqs.q: Are peptides safe?
faqs.a: Yes — when used under medical supervision. At NADZ, peptides are carefully selected, screened, and administered according to best medical practices.

faqs.q: How long does it take to see results?
faqs.a: Most patients experience improvements within 2–6 weeks, depending on the peptide type and therapeutic goals.

faqs.q: Are peptide injections painful?
faqs.a: They are typically quick and minimally uncomfortable. NADZ doctors and nurses ensure gentle, sterile administration.

faqs.q: Do peptides help with anti-aging?
faqs.a: Yes — certain peptides enhance collagen, support tissue repair, improve cellular longevity, and reduce inflammation.

faqs.q: Can peptides help with weight loss?
faqs.a: Some peptides regulate appetite, metabolism, fat burning, insulin sensitivity, and growth-hormone pathways.

faqs.q: Are peptides safe for long-term use?
faqs.a: With proper monitoring, peptides can be used safely for maintenance, cycles, or long-term wellness plans.

faqs.q: Can peptide therapy be combined with other NADZ services?
faqs.a: Absolutely — peptides pair effectively with IV therapy, IHHT, red light, functional medicine, hormonal optimization, and NADZ Blueprint protocols.

faqs.q: Do you offer home-based peptide administration?
faqs.a: Yes. As a home healthcare, NADZ provides full home-based peptide administration. Our DHA-certified doctors visit your home, hotel, or office to ensure safe, sterile, and professionally supervised administration — offering the highest level of privacy, comfort, and convenience while maintaining medical-grade standards.

openGraph.title: NAD+ IV Therapy at Home in Dubai – Premium Cellular Regeneration & Longevity Treatment
openGraph.description: Recharge your cells, enhance brain function, support detoxification, and elevate your overall vitality with doctor-designed NAD+ IV therapy in Dubai.

hero.label: THE FUTURE OF LONGEVITY
hero.title: NAD+ IV Therapy at Home in Dubai – Premium Cellular Regeneration & Longevity Treatment
hero.subtitle: Cellular Energy. Mental Clarity. Longevity — Directly to Your Doorstep.
hero.description: NAD+ (Nicotinamide Adenine Dinucleotide) is one of the body's most essential coenzymes — responsible for cellular energy, DNA repair, metabolism, immunity, and neurological function. As we age, NAD+ levels naturally decline, leading to fatigue, brain fog, slower metabolism, decreased recovery, inflammation, and accelerated aging. NAD+ IV therapy in Dubai replenishes these levels directly into your bloodstream, giving your cells what they need to function at their highest potential. NADZ Home Healthcare brings doctor-led, medical-grade NAD+ therapy to your doorstep with unmatched safety, precision, luxury, and care.

hero.label: ROOT-CAUSE HEALING
hero.title: Functional & Integrated Medicine
hero.subtitle: Your Personalized Pathway to Root-Cause Healing & Lifelong Wellness
hero.description: Functional & Integrated Medicine at NADZ Healthcare goes far beyond symptom management. It is a deep, investigative, biology-first approach that studies how your genetics, environment, lifestyle, metabolism, gut, hormones, nervous system, and immune function interact — so we can identify the true root cause of imbalance and rebuild your health from the inside out. This is medicine for people who want answers, not assumptions — and solutions that actually last.

whatIs.title: What Is Functional & Integrated Medicine?
whatIs.paragraph: Functional & Integrated Medicine is a science-backed, systems-biology method of understanding the body as one interconnected ecosystem. Instead of treating each symptom separately, we study:
bullets: How systems influence one another (gut ↔ brain ↔ endocrine ↔ immune ↔ metabolic)
How stress, sleep, toxins, nutrition, activity & environment shape your biology
How your genes are expressed through lifestyle & biochemical triggers
What your body genuinely needs to heal, restore, and thrive
whatIs.footer: At NADZ, we bring together advanced diagnostics, personalized assessments, and integrative therapeutic strategies to craft a care plan that is uniquely yours — no generic formula, no "one-size-fits-all."

whyChooseFunctionalMedicine.title: Why Choose Functional & Integrated Medicine in Dubai?
whyChooseFunctionalMedicine.description: Because feeling "fine" is not the same as being well. Most chronic symptoms and modern illnesses arise from modifiable factors — inflammation, stress, nutrient deficiencies, sleep disruption, metabolic imbalance, toxin exposure, hormonal disturbance, gut dysfunction.
points: The root causes behind recurring symptoms
How multiple complaints may stem from one imbalance
Why conventional treatments may not have resolved the issue
What your body actually needs to heal & prevent future disease
whyChooseFunctionalMedicine.footer: This approach is ideal for people who want to optimize, prevent, reverse, or enhance their health — not simply cope with their condition.

faqs.q: What conditions does Functional Medicine treat?
faqs.a: Functional Medicine is beneficial for chronic fatigue, gut issues, hormonal imbalance, thyroid disorders, PCOS, autoimmunity, migraines, metabolic issues, inflammation, sleep problems, weight resistance, stress-related conditions, and unexplained symptoms.

faqs.q: How is this different from a regular doctor consultation?
faqs.a: Traditional care aims to manage symptoms. Functional care aims to identify & resolve the root cause.

faqs.q: How long does it take to see improvement?
faqs.a: Most patients see meaningful changes within 4–12 weeks, depending on severity, commitment, and biological factors.

faqs.q: Will I need functional lab tests?
faqs.a: Your doctor will recommend tests based on your symptoms. They are optional but greatly enhance accuracy and personalization.

faqs.q: Can Functional Medicine be combined with conventional treatment?
faqs.a: Yes. It is complementary and can work alongside your existing medical treatments safely.

faqs.q: Is this suitable for longevity and prevention?
faqs.a: Absolutely. Functional Medicine is one of the strongest evidence-based tools for preventing disease and optimizing long-term health span.

faqs.q: Do you offer home-visits for functional assessments?
faqs.a: Yes — NADZ doctors can perform evaluations, sample collection, and follow-ups at your home, hotel, or office.

faqs.q: Is Functional Medicine safe for children?
faqs.a: Yes, when indicated and supervised by a clinician, especially for gut issues, allergies, fatigue, behavioural concerns, or chronic symptoms.

journey.title: Your Care Pathway with NADZ Autonomic Control™
journey.subtitle: At NADZ Healthcare, our Autonomic Control™ sessions support Sleep Disorders Care in Dubai by helping restore balance in the autonomic nervous system (ANS) — the regulator of stress, sleep, and recovery.

steps.title: Initial Assessment
steps.description: A DHA-licensed clinician conducts a private consultation at your home, hotel, or office to understand symptoms like insomnia, awakenings, fatigue, or non-restorative sleep. Together, you define clear goals and expectations.

steps.title: Baseline HRV Snapshot
steps.description: A quick 2-5-minute Heart Rate Variability (HRV) reading gives a snapshot of autonomic balance - the interplay between stress and recovery states - before starting sessions.

steps.title: NADZ Autonomic Control™ Sessions
steps.description: Sub-sensory microcurrent neuromodulation via specific electrodes on wrists and ankles - no pain, no needles, no contractions. Each 60-minute session aims to gently activate parasympathetic recovery pathways and reduce sympathetic over-activity. Sessions are silent, relaxing, and often done while you rest or read at home.

steps.title: Monitoring & Adaptation
steps.description: After a few sessions, your clinician reassesses how you're sleeping, recovering, and feeling. Post-session HRV trends may be shared for educational insight. Frequency is personalized; a common initial cadence is three sessions per week in the first phase.

steps.title: Integration & Support
steps.description: Your doctor may integrate sessions with other aspects of care - medication reviews, lifestyle adjustments, and referrals if needed - with a focus on long-term nervous-system resilience.

ctas: Ask About NADZ Autonomic Control™ for Sleep

whyChoose.title: Why patients choose it with NADZ
items: Non-invasive & imperceptible sessions (no heat, no needles, no contractions)
Delivered by trained clinicians in your home
Integrates seamlessly with ongoing medical care
Clear communication at every step
whyChoose.cta: Ask About NADZ Autonomic Control™ at Home

faqs.q: Is this confidential?
faqs.a: Yes. Everything is delivered discreetly at your location; results are handled with strict privacy.

faqs.q: Is neuromodulation painful or invasive?
faqs.a: No - it's sub-sensory; you won't feel heat, needles, or muscle contractions.

faqs.q: Can I track progress?
faqs.a: Yes - your clinician may use HRV snapshots and simple sleep questionnaires to visualize trends over time.

faqs.q: What sleep issues do you see most often?
faqs.a: Insomnia (difficulty falling or staying asleep), early awakening, non-restorative sleep, and stress-related over-arousal are common concerns. Your doctor assesses symptoms, contributing factors and goals to tailor a plan (home visit, rapid testing, follow-up), and will include non-invasive neuromodulation as part of care.

faqs.q: What is NADZ Autonomic Control non-invasive neuromodulation (XSIGNAL®)?
faqs.a: It's a medical technology that delivers sub-sensory microcurrents designed to gently modulate autonomic nervous system (ANS) activity. Sessions are non-invasive and designed for comfort; the approach is used as a complement to medical care and lifestyle measures.

faqs.q: Will I feel anything during a session?
faqs.a: No. Sessions are imperceptible (no heat, needles, or muscle contractions).

faqs.q: Is it safe? Does it carry certifications?
faqs.a: Yes, the device follows medical-device standards with CE marking and relevant ISO manufacturing certifications.

faqs.q: Who should not receive it? (Contraindications & precautions)
faqs.a: Patients with pacemakers or implanted defibrillators, pregnancy, active oncological processes under certain stages/treatments, acute infectious or febrile processes, active hemorrhages, and other specific clinical scenarios. Your NADZ Home Healthcare doctor will screen for these.

faqs.q: Can it be combined with my current treatments (medication, CBT-I, physiotherapy)?
faqs.a: Yes-it's compatible with other therapies when clinically indicated, except with technologies that apply conflicting electrical currents simultaneously. Your clinician coordinates timing and sequencing.

faqs.q: How are sessions structured?
faqs.a: Assessment, session programming, and periodic review. Sessions are typically planned in consistent blocks; your clinician personalizes cadence and duration for sleep goals.

faqs.q: How many sessions will I need?
faqs.a: Because every person's sleep story is different, the number of sessions isn't fixed. We start with a personalized plan, review your progress regularly, and adapt the schedule to what your body needs most.

faqs.q: Is it done at home, hotel, or clinic?
faqs.a: Our service model support in-home/hotel sessions with trained staff when needed.

faqs.q: What if I'm anxious about devices?
faqs.a: The patient-education FAQs provide scripts and visuals to explain the undetectable nature of the current and the step-by-step process, helping reduce anxiety.

faqs.q: Does XSIGNAL® include heart-rate variability (HRV) tools?
faqs.a: Yes. The dataset describes an included portable ECG and the use of HRV indices for educational monitoring of autonomic balance over time.

faqs.q: Why does HRV matter for sleep?
faqs.a: HRV trends help contextualize stress-recovery balance, which relates to sleep regulation.

faqs.q: Can athletes or busy professionals use it for sleep and recovery?
faqs.a: Yes. The dataset notes adoption in high-performance contexts and daily routines because it's non-invasive and easy to integrate. For sleep-focused goals, sessions are planned around schedules.

faqs.q: Are there side effects?
faqs.a: The dataset centers on safety, imperceptible stimulation, and contraindication screening. Adverse effects are uncommon when used as indicated; your clinician will review your history and monitor tolerance.

faqs.q: Does the current damage cells?
faqs.a: No. XSIGNAL® uses sub-sensory microcurrents at very low voltages and currents (about 3-6 V and up to ?0.9 mA), which are well below levels that could produce thermal or electrical injury to tissues.

faqs.q: What's different vs. other electro- or neuro-stimulation?
faqs.a: XSIGNAL® is positioned in the dataset as a non-invasive, sub-sensory neuromodulation aimed at autonomic regulation, distinct from high-intensity or muscle-contracting electrotherapies.

faqs.q: Can I travel with it or receive sessions while traveling?
faqs.a: Operationally, sessions can be coordinated at your location (home/hotel). Our care model supports privacy and mobility; your clinician will organize logistics

faqs.q: What equipment & accessories are involved?
faqs.a: The dataset lists the medical device, programming, and clinical accessories used by trained staff; patients don't have to manage complex setup

faqs.q: How is progress tracked?
faqs.a: Clinicians may use short HRV snapshots (via the included ECG) and brief sleep questionnaires to educationally visualize trends over time, alongside your subjective sleep reports

cta.tagline: Your journey to better sleep starts with a private, compassionate assessment in the comfort of your own space.

The aim is to support autonomic balance — easing excessive sympathetic drive ("stress response") and favoring parasympathetic recovery. Expected outcomes vary by individual; reported improvements include perceived calm, reduced stress reactivity, better sleep continuity, and overall autonomic support. This approach represents expert Anxiety & Stress Care in Dubai at your convenience.

faqs.a: Assessment, session programming, and periodic review. Sessions are typically planned in consistent blocks; your clinician personalizes cadence and duration for sleep goals.

faqs.q: How many sessions will I need?
faqs.a: Because every person's sleep story is different, the number of sessions isn't fixed. We start with a personalized plan, review your progress regularly, and adapt the schedule to what your body needs most.

faqs.q: Is it done at home, hotel, or clinic?
faqs.a: Our service model support in-home/hotel sessions with trained staff when needed.

faqs.q: What if I'm anxious about devices?
faqs.a: The patient-education FAQs provide scripts and visuals to explain the undetectable nature of the current and the step-by-step process, helping reduce anxiety.

faqs.q: Does XSIGNAL® include heart-rate variability (HRV) tools?
faqs.a: Yes. The dataset describes an included portable ECG and the use of HRV indices for educational monitoring of autonomic balance over time.

faqs.q: Are there side effects?
faqs.a: The dataset centers on safety, imperceptible stimulation, and contraindication screening. Adverse effects are uncommon when used as indicated; your clinician will review your history and monitor tolerance

faqs.q: Does the current damage cells?
faqs.a: No. XSIGNAL® uses sub-sensory microcurrents at very low voltages and currents (about 3–6 V and up to ≤0.9 mA), which are well below levels that could produce thermal or electrical injury to tissues

faqs.q: What's different vs. other electro- or neuro-stimulation?
faqs.a: XSIGNAL® is positioned in the dataset as a non-invasive, sub-sensory neuromodulation aimed at autonomic regulation, distinct from high-intensity or muscle-contracting electrotherapies

faqs.q: Can I travel with it or receive sessions while traveling?
faqs.a: Operationally, sessions can be coordinated at your location (home/hotel). Our care model supports privacy and mobility; your clinician will organize logistics

faqs.q: What equipment & accessories are involved?
faqs.a: The dataset lists the medical device, programming, and clinical accessories used by trained staff; patients don't have to manage complex setup

faqs.q: How is progress tracked?
faqs.a: Clinicians may use short HRV snapshots (via the included ECG) and brief questionnaires to educationally visualize trends over time, alongside your subjective sleep reports

cta.tagline: Experience expert Erectile Dysfunction & Premature Ejaculation Treatment in Dubai with NADZ — discreet, personalized, and delivered where you feel most comfortable.

hero.primaryCta: Call Now for a Discreet Doctor Consultation

whatIs.title: Because Urgency & Frequency Shouldn't Control Your Day
whatIs.paragraph: Overactive bladder (OAB) can mean urgent, frequent trips to the bathroom — sometimes with leakage or night-time waking — disrupting work, sleep and confidence. With NADZ Home Healthcare, you skip crowded clinics. Our Overactive Bladder Care in Dubai brings a DHA-licensed doctor to your home, hotel, or office for a confidential, compassionate assessment and a clear plan tailored to your goals.
whatIs.footer: Your doctor looks at both symptoms and contributors (stress, sleep, fluid/caffeine timing, medications) and creates a plan at your pace — in full privacy.

items.title: Urgency & Frequency
items.description: Sudden urges, going too often (daytime)

items.title: Nocturia
items.description: Waking at night to urinate

items.title: Urgency incontinence
items.description: Leaks associated with sudden urges

items.title: Pelvic floor tension/overactivity
items.description: Stress-related bladder patterns

items.title: Related concerns
items.description: Poor sleep, anxiety/over-arousal, medication review, lifestyle factors

autonomicControl.title: NADZ Autonomic Control™
autonomicControl.subtitle: How XSIGNAL® can help
autonomicControl.description: As part of a comprehensive plan, your clinician may offer NADZ Autonomic Control™, our non-invasive, sub-sensory microcurrent neuromodulation (XSIGNAL®) delivered at home. The aim is to support autonomic balance — easing excessive sympathetic drive (stress response) and favoring parasympathetic recovery. In bladder care, a calmer autonomic tone may help with urgency perception, pelvic-floor guarding, and night-time awakenings as part of whole-body support. (Individual results vary.)

whoCanBenefit.title: When to Request a Visit
items: Urgency/frequency or leaks are affecting your work, sleep, or daily life
You prefer discreet, at-home evaluation and treatment
You want rapid testing and an actionable plan without clinic wait times
You've completed treatment elsewhere and want structured follow-up
whoCanBenefit.footer: Your NADZ Home Healthcare doctor focuses on both the symptom and the likely contributors, then creates a plan that matches your goals - at your pace and in full privacy.

discreetCare.title: Discreet Care, Wherever You Are
discreetCare.description: Think of this as doctor-at-your-doorstep for overactive bladder. We provide:
items: Thorough clinical assessment and private discussion of symptoms and goals
Tailored treatment plan and prescriptions when appropriate
Guidance on whether further tests or specialist referral are needed
Coordination with labs, pharmacy delivery, nursing, or physiotherapy if required
Clear follow-up so you're supported from first visit to results
discreetCare.cta: Book a Private Home or Hotel Visit

fastTesting.title: Fast Screening & Clear Next Steps
fastTesting.description: When indicated, we use MOHAP-certified rapid point-of-care (POC) screens and coordinate DHA-approved lab tests (e.g., infection markers, glucose, thyroid) to rule out contributors — helping you move from uncertainty to action quickly. Accurate. Discreet. At your location.

visitIncludes.title: What Your Discreet Visit Includes
items: Same-day or urgent visits — evenings, nights, weekends (24/7)
Detailed history and examination, with time to answer your questions
Clear plan; prescriptions when appropriate
Recommendations for tests and on-the-spot POC where indicated
Coordination with labs, pharmacy delivery, and home nursing/physiotherapy

journey.title: Your Care Pathway with NADZ Autonomic Control™ for Overactive Bladder Care in Dubai
journey.subtitle: We mirror the same patient-friendly pathway used across your service pages for consistency and trust.

steps.title: Initial Assessment
steps.description: Private consultation at your home, hotel, or office to understand bladder patterns (day/night frequency, urgency, leaks), fluid/caffeine timing, sleep and stress. Expectations are set together.

steps.title: Baseline HRV Snapshot
steps.description: A quick 2–5-minute Heart Rate Variability (HRV) reading offers an educational snapshot of autonomic balance (stress vs. recovery) before sessions begin.

steps.title: NADZ Autonomic Control™ Sessions
steps.description: Sub-sensory microcurrent neuromodulation via electrodes on wrists and ankles — no pain, no needles, no contractions. Each 60-minute session is quiet and relaxing, often while you rest at home.

steps.title: Monitoring & Adaptation
steps.description: After a few sessions, your clinician reviews how you're feeling and functioning. HRV trends may be shared for educational insight. Frequency is personalized; a common initial cadence is three sessions per week in the first phase.

steps.title: Integration & Support
steps.description: We integrate sessions with other aspects of care (medication review, pelvic-floor physio, lifestyle measures), focusing on long-term nervous-system resilience.

ctas: Ask About NADZ Autonomic Control™ for OAB

whyChoose.title: Why patients choose it with NADZ
items: Non-invasive & imperceptible sessions (no heat, no needles, no contractions)
Delivered by trained clinicians in your home
Integrates seamlessly with ongoing medical care (urology/urogyn, pelvic physio)
Clear communication at every step
whyChoose.cta: Ask About NADZ Autonomic Control™ at Home

faqs.q: Is this confidential?
faqs.a: Yes. Everything is delivered discreetly at your location; records are handled with strict privacy.

faqs.q: Is neuromodulation painful or invasive?
faqs.a: No. It's sub-sensory; you won't feel heat, needles, or muscle contractions.

faqs.q: Does the current damage cells?
faqs.a: No. XSIGNAL® uses very low-intensity microcurrents, designed to avoid thermal or electrical tissue injury. The focus is gentle autonomic modulation, not muscle contraction.

faqs.q: How many sessions will I need?
faqs.a: There's no one-size-fits-all number. We start with a personalized plan, review progress regularly, and adapt the schedule to your goals and response.

faqs.q: Can it work alongside my current treatments (medication, pelvic physio, urology)?
faqs.a: Yes. It's meant to complement your existing care when clinically indicated; we coordinate timing and sequencing.

faqs.q: Who should not receive it? (Contraindications & precautions)
faqs.a: We screen for pacemakers/ICDs, pregnancy, acute febrile illness, active hemorrhage, and other specific scenarios before starting.

faqs.q: Can I track progress?
faqs.a: Yes. Brief HRV snapshots and short bladder diaries/questionnaires can help visualize trends over time, alongside your symptom reports.

faqs.q: Is it available while I'm traveling?
faqs.a: Yes. We can coordinate sessions at your home or hotel for continuity and privacy.
